# Reliability Checklist: Search, Accessibility, Deep Links, Docs Validation

**Purpose**: Validate DOC-010 reliability requirement completeness, clarity, consistency, and measurable review evidence for local docs validation, generated-reference drift checks, conditional PR Checks behavior, and smoke artifacts.
**Created**: 2026-06-19
**Feature**: [spec.md](../spec.md)

**Note**: This checklist is generated by the `/speckit-checklist` command based on feature context and requirements.

## Requirement Completeness

- [ ] CHK001 Does the spec define one complete local docs validation command that includes generated reference checks, site checks, build/link validation, safe-aids validation, docs-quality validation, and browser smoke? [Completeness, Spec §FR-007, Spec §FR-008, Plan §Testing]
- [ ] CHK002 Does the plan list focused iteration commands so maintainers can isolate generated reference, site, safe-aids, docs-quality, and smoke failures without creating a second required validation path? [Completeness, Plan §Testing, Quickstart §Focused Iteration Commands]
- [ ] CHK003 Does the spec define generated-reference source input ownership clearly enough to prevent stale reference pages after source changes? [Completeness, Spec §Generated Reference Source Input, Contract §Docs Validation Path]
- [ ] CHK004 Does the PR Checks contract define rendered docs-site, generated-reference source, docs-validation contract, and plugin-only changed-file surfaces? [Completeness, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]
- [x] CHK005 Does the browser smoke contract define the smoke artifact name and contents and quantify the 7-day retention expectation? [Resolved, Spec §FR-013, Spec §Assumptions, Contract §Browser Smoke]

## Requirement Clarity

- [ ] CHK006 Is `validate-docs` specified as a stable PR Checks gate that uses job-level changed-file detection rather than workflow-level path filters? [Clarity, Spec §FR-009, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]
- [ ] CHK007 Is the no-docs-change skip behavior specified as a successful, visible skip rather than a missing or pending required check? [Clarity, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]
- [ ] CHK008 Are Playwright baseURL responsibilities clear enough to keep smoke route assertions logical while supporting the deployed `/racecraft-plugins-public` prefix? [Clarity, Spec §FR-010, Contract §Browser Smoke]
- [ ] CHK009 Are browser smoke assertions scoped to representative route/deep-link/search checks rather than broad screenshots or full visual snapshots? [Clarity, Spec §Browser Smoke Route Rationale, Contract §Browser Smoke]
- [ ] CHK010 Are forbidden automated validation actions stated clearly enough to prevent live plugin installs, local-user-file inspection, browser-side local command execution, analytics, or destructive commands? [Clarity, Spec §FR-011, Quickstart §Forbidden In Automated Validation]

## Requirement Consistency

- [ ] CHK011 Do the spec, plan, quickstart, and contracts all name `pnpm --dir docs-site validate` as the single complete local validation path? [Consistency, Spec §SC-005, Plan §Testing, Quickstart §Local Validation Path, Contract §Docs Validation Path]
- [ ] CHK012 Do generated-reference consistency requirements align across `reference:check`, deterministic link/anchor validation, and generated source-input detection? [Consistency, Spec §FR-003, Spec §FR-007, Data Model §Generated Reference Source Input]
- [ ] CHK013 Do docs-site PR Checks requirements preserve existing `detect`, `test`, and `validate-plugins` plugin matrix semantics while adding `validate-docs`? [Consistency, Plan §Summary, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]
- [x] CHK014 Does DOC-010 explicitly require docs-site-only PRs to run docs validation without forcing unrelated plugin matrix jobs? [Resolved, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]

## Acceptance Criteria Quality

- [ ] CHK015 Can SC-005 be objectively judged from local command output and PR Checks status names without relying on reviewer inference? [Measurability, Spec §SC-005]
- [ ] CHK016 Can smoke evidence completeness be objectively judged from the route list, viewport list, search smoke, deep-link samples, and artifact name? [Measurability, Spec §SC-006, Contract §Browser Smoke]
- [ ] CHK017 Can reviewers trace validation evidence back to changed docs-site surfaces, known gaps, manual review notes, and rollback/fallback notes? [Measurability, Spec §FR-013, Spec §PR Review Packet Requirements]

## Scenario Coverage

- [ ] CHK018 Are docs-site content/config/script changes, generated-reference source changes, docs-validation contract changes, and plugin-only changes all covered by distinct CI expectations? [Coverage, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]
- [ ] CHK019 Are failure modes for stale generated references, broken links or anchors, unsafe aid regressions, and smoke failures covered with actionable output expectations? [Coverage, Contract §Docs Validation Path]
- [ ] CHK020 Are manual accessibility review notes and Playwright smoke artifacts both required without implying automation certifies complete accessibility? [Coverage, Spec §Accessibility Review Requirements, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary]

## Notes

- Focus areas selected: conditional docs-site PR Checks behavior, deterministic local validation commands, generated reference consistency, link validation, Playwright smoke artifacts, and docs-only PR isolation from plugin matrix behavior.
- Depth level: Standard reviewer gate.
- Actor/timing: PR reviewer before implementation tasks begin.

## Re-run Verification

- [x] CHK021 Is the smoke artifact retention now quantified as 7 days across the spec, browser-smoke contract, PR Checks contract, and quickstart? [Resolved, Spec §FR-013, Contract §Browser Smoke, Quickstart §CI Expectations]
- [x] CHK022 Is docs-site-only PR isolation from unrelated plugin matrix jobs now explicit across the spec, plan, PR Checks contract, and quickstart? [Resolved, Spec §FR-009, Plan §Phase 0 Research Summary, Contract §PR Checks Docs Gate]
- [x] CHK023 Does the Reliability domain now contain no remaining gap markers after remediation? [Resolved, Checklist §Requirement Completeness, Checklist §Requirement Consistency]
